comment: Labels identical ('Paper') and both are modeled as subclasses of Document; high-confidence lexical equivalence.
Same label 'Review' and both are subclasses of Document in the two ontologies.
Same label 'Conference' and similar modeling (event/edition of a conference).
Same label 'Person' and matching disjoint/role modeling across ontologies.
Both vocabularies define 'Document' with similar usage (parent for Paper, Review, etc.).
Same label 'Presenter' and both are subclass-of Person/Conference_Participant in respective ontologies.
Exact lexical match 'Workshop' and same role as an event type in both ontologies.
Names differ only by underscore; both model session concepts related to conferences (subclass of Session/Event).
Accepted paper classes with near-identical meaning; lexical difference is underscore/compound naming.
Both define a 'Rejected' paper class used in evaluation pipelines; lexical forms closely match.
Concepts are similar (author of papers) though EKAW uses the compound name 'Paper_Author'; moderate lexical normalization required.
Both are relations connecting Papers/Events to topic concepts (Topic vs Research_Topic). Structural similarity in domain/range motivated the mapping.
Both properties express the authorship relation between Document/Paper and Person; labels and inverse names align (isWrittenBy <-> writtenBy).
